name: memory_stream_like
description: A memory-bandwidth-oriented benchmark that performs repeated reads and writes over a large array, similar in spirit to STREAM-like kernels.

semantic_roles.inner_iters: repetition of memory access per element
semantic_roles.iterations: number of repeated kernel launches for averaging

expected_behavior.small_n: runtime dominated by launch overhead and fixed costs
expected_behavior.medium_n: transition region where bandwidth begins to dominate
expected_behavior.large_n: runtime scales with memory bandwidth and saturates at hardware limit

performance_signals.memory_bound: runtime increases with n but is insensitive to inner_iters
performance_signals.bandwidth_saturation: runtime per element stabilizes for large n
performance_signals.block_sensitivity: performance varies with threads_per_block if occupancy or scheduling matters

derived_metrics.effective_bandwidth_gb_s: computed from total bytes transferred divided by runtime

known_issues: very small n dominated by launch overhead
very large n may exceed GPU memory limits
checksum does not guarantee full correctness
